description: Phase 2 of 2 — DDGC Conditional Effects, Damage Variance, And Hit Resolution. Start only after Phase 1 is complete. This PRD closes the remaining semantic-policy gaps by implementing DDGC-specific conditional evaluation, deterministic/rolled damage policy seams, and a real accuracy-vs-dodge resolution path.

userStories.title: P2-0: Inventory open conditional and hit-policy gaps
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a concrete inventory of remaining DDGC conditional rules and hit-resolution behaviors so Phase 2 targets the highest-value semantic gaps first.
acceptanceCriteria: A checked-in note or progress entry lists the migrated skills or statuses still blocked by DDGC-specific conditions, damage variance, or dodge-aware hit resolution
The inventory distinguishes framework-native conditions from DDGC-only conditions
The inventory selects a small initial condition subset and at least one hit-policy anchor scenario for implementation
userStories.notes: Discovery slice for B-004, B-006, and B-010. Inventory created at tasks/phase2_condition_hit_policy_inventory.md.

userStories.title: P2-1: Add condition evaluation context model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a game-layer condition evaluation context so DDGC-specific checks can read actor, target, turn, and encounter state through one stable interface.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated DDGC condition-evaluation context type exists outside framework crates
The context exposes enough data for actor-side, target-side, turn-state, and encounter-state conditions
A focused test proves the context can be created deterministically from an in-progress combat state

userStories.title: P2-2: Bridge framework-native conditions through the DDGC adapter
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the DDGC evaluation layer to pass through supported framework-native conditions so one adapter can handle both generic and DDGC-specific checks.
acceptanceCriteria: The DDGC adapter can evaluate at least one existing framework-native condition through the new shared interface
The bridge does not duplicate framework logic unnecessarily
A focused test proves supported native conditions behave the same before and after the adapter layer
userStories.notes: Compatibility slice before DDGC-only condition work.

userStories.title: P2-3: Surface unsupported conditions explicitly
userStories.description: As a developer, I need unsupported DDGC conditions to fail loudly in the game layer so missing semantic support is visible instead of silently flattening behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Unsupported condition kinds are surfaced via explicit error, trace marker, or ledgered fallback path
The combat path does not silently apply or ignore an effect when a required DDGC condition is unknown
A focused test proves unsupported conditions are observable in a deterministic way
userStories.notes: Guardrail slice before incremental condition implementation.

userStories.title: P2-4: Implement first-round condition semantics
userStories.description: As a developer, I need FirstRound-style DDGC conditions implemented so opening-turn-only effects stop behaving as if they were unconditional.
acceptanceCriteria: The game layer can evaluate whether the current action occurs on the first round or not
At least one migrated or fixture-backed effect uses the new first-round condition successfully
A deterministic test proves the effect applies on round one and stops applying afterwards
userStories.notes: Choose one high-leverage DDGC condition and land it fully.

userStories.title: P2-5: Implement stress-threshold condition semantics
userStories.description: As a developer, I need StressAbove or StressBelow DDGC conditions implemented so stress-driven behavior can diverge correctly instead of being always-on.
acceptanceCriteria: The game layer can evaluate at least one stress-threshold condition using DDGC stress state
At least one migrated or fixture-backed effect proves stress-threshold behavior end-to-end
A deterministic test proves the same effect changes outcome across the threshold boundary

userStories.title: P2-6: Implement one additional DDGC-only condition family
userStories.description: As a developer, I need one more DDGC-only condition family implemented so the adapter proves it can scale beyond a single special case.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one additional DDGC-only condition family beyond FirstRound and stress-threshold is implemented
The condition is exercised by a migrated or fixture-backed skill or status
A focused test proves the new condition integrates through the shared adapter instead of a one-off branch
userStories.notes: Pick the most reused remaining condition after the Phase 2 inventory.

userStories.title: P2-7: Update semantic gap ledger for conditional support
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the semantic-gap and blocker ledgers updated as condition support lands so the repo no longer describes closed gaps as entirely deferred.
acceptanceCriteria: SEMANTIC_GAPS.md reflects the newly implemented DDGC condition support accurately
MIGRATION_BLOCKERS.md is updated if B-004 can be partially or fully reclassified
The documentation distinguishes remaining unsupported conditions from the ones now implemented
userStories.notes: Documentation closeout for the B-004 subset delivered in Phase 2.

userStories.title: P2-8: Add deterministic damage policy interface
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a game-layer damage policy interface so fixed-average and rolled-damage behavior can coexist without rewriting every migrated skill definition.
acceptanceCriteria: Game-layer combat code exposes a clear policy seam for translating DDGC damage ranges into resolved damage values
Existing deterministic test paths continue using a stable default policy
A focused test proves policy selection occurs through one shared interface rather than scattered branches

userStories.title: P2-9: Preserve fixed-average deterministic mode
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the current fixed-average damage behavior preserved as an explicit deterministic mode so golden traces stay stable while variance support is added.
acceptanceCriteria: Fixed-average damage resolution is represented as an explicit selectable policy
Existing deterministic traces or regression scenarios continue to pass unchanged under that policy
A focused test proves average-mode output remains stable across repeated runs
userStories.notes: Do not introduce variance into default test mode in this slice.

userStories.title: P2-10: Add rolled-damage policy for DDGC ranges
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a rolled-damage policy for DDGC min/max ranges so the game can eventually restore variance without abandoning deterministic testing.
acceptanceCriteria: The game layer can resolve DDGC min/max ranges through a non-average policy path
The rolled-damage path is explicitly selectable and does not replace deterministic default behavior
A focused test proves the rolled policy stays within the expected range bounds
userStories.notes: Second B-006 slice after the policy seam exists.

userStories.title: P2-11: Add hit-resolution context model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a DDGC-specific hit-resolution context so accuracy, dodge, and related combat modifiers can be evaluated coherently through one policy seam.
acceptanceCriteria: Game-layer combat code exposes a dedicated context for hit-resolution decisions
The context can read attacker accuracy, defender dodge, and any required supporting state
A focused test proves the context is deterministic and reusable across repeated hit checks

userStories.title: P2-12: Implement accuracy-vs-dodge policy
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an actual accuracy-vs-dodge resolution policy so DDGC skills can hit or miss under game-layer control instead of relying only on coarse probability placeholders.
acceptanceCriteria: A DDGC-specific hit policy resolves at least one dodge-sensitive attack path end-to-end
The policy integrates with the shared hit-resolution context rather than a one-off helper
A deterministic or seeded test proves hit and miss outcomes can both be exercised intentionally

userStories.title: P2-13: Add regression coverage for conditions and hit policies
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Phase 2 mechanics locked by regression tests so later content migration can rely on conditional effects, damage variance seams, and hit resolution without re-opening the same gaps.
acceptanceCriteria: Regression tests cover implemented DDGC-specific conditions, deterministic damage mode, rolled-damage policy seams, and accuracy-vs-dodge behavior
The test suite uses game_ddgc_headless public APIs and fixture content rather than framework internals
cargo test --manifest-path Cargo.toml exits 0 with full Phase 2 coverage enabled
userStories.notes: Phase 2 closeout story. Start only after Phase 1 is complete.
